skill.name: id-photo-tool
skill.description: Replace an ID photo background color (blue/white/red) and/or add a configurable text watermark when you need compliant ID-photo variants from local JPG/PNG files.
skill.summary: A local-only Python tool that changes ID photo backgrounds to blue, white, or red and adds text watermarks with customizable position, opacity, and font size.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 27 static findings are dismissed as false positives. The scanner confused argparse .keys() calls with cryptographic keys, markdown code fences with shell injection, and source URL citations with network access. The actual code is a benign local image processing tool using Pillow and numpy. It has no network access, no credential handling, no command injection vectors, and no dangerous imports. All processing is local and only accesses user-provided file paths.

content.user_title: Create Compliant ID Photos with Background and Watermark Tools
content.value_statement: Official ID photo submissions often require specific background colors and visible watermarks. This skill changes photo backgrounds to blue, white, or red and adds configurable text watermarks, all processed locally on your machine without uploading images anywhere.
actual_capabilities: Replace ID photo background color with blue, white, or red using automatic color-distance detection
Add configurable text watermark at five positions with adjustable opacity, font size, and font color
Process JPG and PNG files without overwriting the original input file
Detect dominant background color automatically by sampling edge pixels
Keep output format matching the input format (JPG or PNG)
Prevent accidental overwrite by requiring an explicit output path different from the input
limitations: Only supports three target background colors: blue, white, and red
Background replacement uses simple color-distance matching, not AI-based subject segmentation
Cannot process image formats other than JPG and PNG
Requires Python 3.x with Pillow and numpy installed locally

use_cases.title: ID Photo for Government Applications
use_cases.description: A job applicant needs to change a personal photo background to blue for a passport application. The tool processes the photo locally, keeping the image private and secure.

use_cases.title: Watermark ID Photos for Verification
use_cases.description: An HR department adds a 'For verification only' watermark to employee ID photos before sharing them with a third-party background check service.

use_cases.title: Batch Photo Standardization
use_cases.description: A school administrator converts student photos to have white backgrounds and consistent watermark text for student ID cards.

prompt_templates.title: Change Photo Background to Blue
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the background of my photo at ~/photo.jpg to blue and save it to ~/photo_blue.jpg
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple background color replacement for a single photo

prompt_templates.title: Add Watermark to ID Photo
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a 'For official use only' watermark to my photo at ~/photo.jpg and save it to ~/photo_watermarked.jpg
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a visible watermark to protect shared ID photos

prompt_templates.title: Change Background and Add Watermark in Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: First change the background of ~/photo.jpg to white, then add a watermark that says 'Application copy' at the bottom-right. Save the result to ~/photo_final.jpg
prompt_templates.scenario: Combining background replacement and watermark operations

prompt_templates.title: Custom Watermark with Specific Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a watermark to ~/input.png that says 'Sample - Not for ID use' in red color at the center position with 0.5 opacity and 36px font size. Save to ~/output.png
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced watermark customization with specific visual parameters

output_examples.input: I need to change my passport photo background from white to blue
output_examples.output: I will process your photo using the ID photo tool. The tool detects the current white background by sampling edge pixels and replaces it with blue (RGB 0, 102, 204). Your original file will not be modified. Please provide the path to your photo file.

output_examples.input: Add a watermark saying 'For verification only' to my ID photo
output_examples.output: I will add a text watermark to your photo. By default the watermark appears at the bottom-right position with 0.3 opacity in white color. The watermark text is composited onto the image as a semi-transparent overlay. Your original file is not overwritten. Please provide the input and output file paths.

output_examples.input: Can you process this photo and make it suitable for a job application?
output_examples.output: Yes, I can change the background to white or blue and add a watermark. The tool supports both JPG and PNG formats. I will process the photo locally without sending it over the internet. Please share the input file path, desired background color, and whether you need a watermark added.

best_practices: Always provide a different output path from the input path to prevent accidental overwrite of the original photo
Use high-resolution source photos with uniform backgrounds for the best color replacement results
Process sensitive ID photos only on your local machine since this tool has no network access and keeps data private
anti_patterns: Do not use this tool for complex backgrounds with multiple colors or busy patterns as it uses simple color-distance matching
Do not attempt to process image formats other than JPG or PNG as they are not supported by the tool
Do not use this tool for AI-powered background removal or subject segmentation as it does not use machine learning

faq.question: What background colors does this tool support?
faq.answer: The tool supports three colors: blue (RGB 0, 102, 204), white (RGB 255, 255, 255), and red (RGB 204, 0, 0).

faq.question: Does the tool overwrite my original photo?
faq.answer: No. The tool requires you to provide an explicit output path and validates that the output path is different from the input path.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The tool supports JPG and PNG formats only. The output format matches the input format automatically.

faq.question: Does this tool send my photos over the internet?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens locally on your machine. The tool has no network access and your images never leave your computer.

faq.question: Can I change the watermark position and appearance?
faq.answer: Yes. You can set the position (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right, center), opacity, font size, and font color.

faq.question: What Python packages do I need to install?
faq.answer: You need Python 3.x with the Pillow and numpy packages installed. Run: python -m pip install pillow numpy
